course.title: Lesson 30: The Death of a Ghost

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translations.zh-Hans: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: Why did the two brothers keep the secret?

translations.zh-Hans: 兄弟二人为什么保守这个秘密？

segments.text: For years, villagers believed that Endley Farm was haunted.

translations.zh-Hans: 多年来，村民们一直认为恩德利农场闹鬼。

segments.text: The farm was owned by two brothers, Joe and Bob Cox.

translations.zh-Hans: 农场归乔·考克斯和鲍勃·考克斯兄弟所有。

segments.text: They employed a few farm hands, but no one was willing to work there long.

translations.zh-Hans: 他们雇了几个农场工人，但没有人愿意在那里长期干下去。

segments.text: Every time a worker gave up his job, he told the same story.

translations.zh-Hans: 每当工人辞职时，讲的都是同一个故事。

segments.text: Farm laborers said that they always woke up to find that work had been done overnight.

translations.zh-Hans: 农场工人说，他们每天醒来都会发现有人在夜间把活干完了。

segments.text: Hay had been cut and cow sheds had been cleaned.

translations.zh-Hans: 干草割好了，牛棚也打扫干净了。

segments.text: A farm worker, who stayed up all night, claimed to have seen a figure cutting corn in the moonlight.

translations.zh-Hans: 一名彻夜未眠的农场工人声称，曾看见一个人影在月光下割庄稼。

segments.text: In time, it became an accepted fact that the Cox brothers employed a conscientious ghost that did most of their work for them.

translations.zh-Hans: 久而久之，人们公认考克斯兄弟雇了一个勤劳的鬼，替他们干了大部分农活。

segments.text: No one suspected that there might be someone else on the farm who had never been seen.

translations.zh-Hans: 没有人怀疑农场里可能还住着一个从未露面的人。

segments.text: This was indeed the case.

translations.zh-Hans: 事实的确如此。

segments.text: A short time ago, villagers were astonished to learn that the ghost of Endley had died.

translations.zh-Hans: 不久前，村民们惊讶地得知，恩德利农场的“鬼”死了。

segments.text: Everyone went to the funeral, for the ghost was none other than Eric Cox, a third brother who was supposed to have died as a young man.

translations.zh-Hans: 所有人都去参加葬礼，因为这个鬼不是别人，正是他们的三弟埃里克·考克斯；人们一直以为他年轻时就死了。

segments.text: After the funeral, Joe and Bob revealed a secret which they had kept for over fifty years.

translations.zh-Hans: 葬礼后，乔和鲍勃揭开了一个保守50多年的秘密。

segments.text: Eric had been the eldest son of the family, very much older than his two brothers.

translations.zh-Hans: 埃里克是家中长子，比两个弟弟年长许多。

segments.text: He had been obliged to join the army during the Second World War.

translations.zh-Hans: 第二次世界大战期间，他被迫参军。

segments.text: As he hated army life, he decided to desert his regiment.

translations.zh-Hans: 由于厌恶军旅生活，他决定逃离部队。

segments.text: When he learnt that he would be sent abroad, he returned to the farm and his father hid him until the end of the war.

translations.zh-Hans: 得知自己将被派往国外后，他回到农场，父亲把他藏到战争结束。

segments.text: Fearing the authorities, remained in hiding after the war as well.

translations.zh-Hans: 由于害怕当局追究，他在战后仍继续躲藏。

segments.text: His father told everybody that Eric had been killed in action.

translations.zh-Hans: 父亲告诉所有人，埃里克已经战死。

segments.text: The only other people who knew the secret were Joe and Bob.

translations.zh-Hans: 除父亲外，只有乔和鲍勃知道这个秘密。

segments.text: They did not even tell their wives.

translations.zh-Hans: 他们甚至没有告诉各自的妻子。

segments.text: When their father died, they thought it their duty to keep Eric in hiding.

translations.zh-Hans: 父亲去世后，他们认为自己有责任继续把埃里克藏起来。

segments.text: All these years, Eric had lived as a recluse.

translations.zh-Hans: 这些年来，埃里克一直过着隐居生活。

segments.text: He used to sleep during the day and work at night, quite unaware of the fact that he had become the ghost of Endley.

translations.zh-Hans: 他白天睡觉、夜间干活，完全不知道自己已经成了“恩德利农场的鬼”。

segments.text: When he died, however, his brothers found it impossible to keep the secret any longer.

translations.zh-Hans: 然而他去世后，兄弟二人再也无法继续保守秘密。
